perf: switch devcontainer from universal (~10GB) to base:ubuntu (~200MB)

The universal:2 image is ~10GB and includes Java, .NET, Go, Ruby, PHP,
etc — none of which this workshop uses. Then we were re-installing
Python, Node, PowerShell, and Azure CLI as features on top of what
universal already shipped.

we actually need. Also:
- Combine pip install into single call with --quiet flag
- Remove redundant remoteUser (base image uses vscode by default)
- Remove pwsh as default terminal (not all participants prefer it)

commit.message:
Fix SQLite write slowdown caused by concurrent reads

Root cause: WAL auto-checkpoint triggered by read connections (sqlite_api
and external Grafana) blocked the writer, dropping throughput from 60K
to 234 rows/s.

Fixes:
- Disable wal_autocheckpoint on both writer and query connections
- Manual WAL checkpoint on shutdown (in create_analysis_indexes)
- Add cache_size to query connection for faster reads
- Increase shutdown timeout to 30s

Charts: Fix Line Chart Annotations Custom story error in Storybook (#47990)

* Charts: Replace gridicons with inline SVG to fix Storybook Vite transform error

The gridicons package is CJS-only, which causes Vite's CJS-to-ESM interop
in Storybook to fail when resolving the default export. This results in
Gridicon being treated as an object instead of a React component, throwing
'Element type is invalid... got: object' in LineChartAnnotationLabelWithPopover.

Replace the single Gridicon usage (cross icon in popover close button) with
an inline SVG component using the same path data. This eliminates the
CJS interop issue and also removes the gridicons dependency (~106KB) from
the charts package.

Ref CHARTS-208

* Add changelog entry for CHARTS-208 fix

* Charts: Use @wordpress/icons instead of inline SVG for close icon

Replace the inline CloseIcon SVG with the close icon from @wordpress/icons
to align with WordPress ecosystem conventions. The package is tree-shakeable
and already used widely across the monorepo.

* Update changelog entry to reflect @wordpress/icons usage

* Update changelog

commit.message:
Two-period spending money with 3-day bill lead time

Overhaul useSpendingMoney to look across two pay periods:
- Period 1: checking balance minus bills due before next paycheck
- + Paycheck incoming
- Period 2: minus bills due before second paycheck
- Budget items prorated across the full window
- = Free Spending

Bills use a 3-day lead time for period slotting — a bill due on
the 1st is effectively slotted into the EOM pay period (due date
minus 3 days = 28th/29th). This ensures the mortgage and similar
bills are attributed to the paycheck that actually needs to cover
them, not the one after the due date.

Update SpendingMoneyBreakdown to show both periods separately
with individual bill line items, the incoming paycheck, and the
budget waterfall.

commit.message:
Add descriptions and progressive hint spoilers to all practice problems

Each of the 19 practice problems now has:
- A brief description of what the problem tests
- A collapsible 'Hints' spoiler with 3-4 progressive hints
  (from high-level approach to specific implementation details)

Covers: Beginner (sum, min, XOR), Lazy Propagation (range add/set,
circular, XOR on segment), Counting/Inversions (smaller after self,
reverse pairs, salary queries, nested ranges), Custom Merge (max
subarray, brackets, GCD+count, function composition), Persistent/
Offline (kth smallest, distinct values, DSU on tree).

Also added .problem-desc CSS for muted description text.

commit.message:
[MINOR][Tests] New Lab API Changes (#1707)

This pull request adds support for loading test account and application
configurations from Key Vault JSON files, enabling faster and more
reliable UI test setup by bypassing the Lab API when possible. It
introduces new providers for Key Vault-backed credentials, accounts, and
app configs, and updates the UI test infrastructure to use these
providers as a first option, with fallback to the Lab API. It also
improves certificate handling for passwordless scenarios.

**Key Vault integration for UI test infrastructure:**

* Added new files and build targets for `MSIDKeyVaultAccountProvider`,
`MSIDKeyVaultCredentialProvider`, and `MSIDKeyVaultAppConfigProvider` to
the Xcode project, including their headers and implementations.

## Proposed changes

Added way to retrieve account upns from json, as well as test apps to
align with new lab api's. Also, added capability for pipeline to
dynamically retrieve the lab cert instead of having to update the
conf.json.

Changes are necessary because in the future, account upn api we use now
will not be supported, every team has to use a json with all the account
upns they use. Same with the app id api, have to use a json as the api
will go away.

Improvement was made to use cert from keyvault instead of the conf.json
first since the cert will expire frequently
